Title: In vivo assembly of the sorgoleone biosynthetic pathway and its impact on agroinfiltrated leaves of Nicotiana benthamiana

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: phytotoxic allelochemical; inhibits photosystem II/photosynthetic electron flow; contributes to weed suppression
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: palmitoleoyl-CoA-related C16 fatty acyl precursor pool
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: desaturated alkyl side-chain precursor for downstream alkylresorcinol formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: introduces double bond(s) into the C16 fatty acyl side-chain precursor used for sorgoleone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required as part of the five-gene pathway for production of sorgoleone in agroinfiltrated N. benthamiana leaves and associated necrotic lesions; omission prevented full lesion phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Agrobacterium-mediated transient coexpression in N. benthamiana leaves; combinatorial pathway reconstruction with partial constructs; RT-qPCR confirmation of transgene expression; GC/MS detection of sorgoleone; lesion phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in agroinfiltrated leaves; detectable by RT-qPCR as early as 2 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in leaves; lesion monitored from 3-6 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: constitutive promoters; different promoters/terminators used in multigene construct to avoid interference/silencing
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of agroinfiltrated N. benthamiana leaves, NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA660791
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbDES2 was included as one of five Sorghum genes required to assemble the sorgoleone pathway in planta. 2. Coexpression with SbDES3, SbARS2, SbOMT3, and CYP71AM1 enabled sorgoleone accumulation detected by GC/MS in N. benthamiana leaves. 3. Constructs lacking the desaturase module did not produce the pronounced necrotic phenotype associated with the complete pathway, indicating the desaturation steps are needed for authentic sorgoleone formation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: expression as part of the complete pathway caused severe phytotoxicity/necrotic lesions in host leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for pathway stacking to engineer de novo sorgoleone production, but expression should likely be restricted spatially, e.g. root hair-specific promoters, to avoid host phytotoxicity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion notes that none of the five sorgoleone biosynthetic enzymes possess chloroplast transit peptides; pathway may operate in ER using endogenous C16:1 substrate, but exact reaction site remains unresolved

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: phytotoxic allelochemical; inhibits photosystem II/photosynthetic electron flow; contributes to weed suppression
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: desaturated C16 fatty acyl precursor in sorgoleone side-chain formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: further desaturated alkyl side-chain precursor for downstream alkylresorcinol formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: introduces additional double bond(s) into the fatty acyl side chain required for the sorgoleone structure
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required as part of the complete five-gene pathway for sorgoleone accumulation and strong necrotic lesions in N. benthamiana; omission prevented full phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Agrobacterium-mediated transient coexpression in N. benthamiana leaves; combinatorial pathway reconstruction with partial constructs; RT-qPCR confirmation of transgene expression; GC/MS detection of sorgoleone; lesion phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in agroinfiltrated leaves; detectable by RT-qPCR as early as 2 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in leaves; lesion monitored from 3-6 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: constitutive promoters; different promoters/terminators used in multigene construct to avoid interference/silencing
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of agroinfiltrated N. benthamiana leaves, NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA660791
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbDES3 was coassembled with the other four sorghum genes in a multigene vector for in vivo pathway reconstruction. 2. Full-pathway expression generated sorgoleone detected by GC/MS, whereas partial constructs lacking the desaturase module did not yield the full toxic phenotype. 3. SbDES3 therefore functions as a necessary upstream enzyme for authentic sorgoleone biosynthesis in the heterologous host.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: expression as part of the complete pathway caused severe phytotoxicity/necrotic lesions in host leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: valuable for complete pathway engineering of sorgoleone, but tissue-restricted expression is advisable to minimize autotoxicity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Construct lacking SbDES2/SbDES3 but containing SbARS2, SbOMT3, and CYP71AM1 caused only slight lesion-like symptoms, likely from analogs made from endogenous substrates rather than authentic sorgoleone

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: phytotoxic allelochemical; inhibits photosystem II/photosynthetic electron flow; contributes to weed suppression
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: desaturated fatty acyl-CoA starter for alkylresorcinol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: alkylresorcinol intermediate for downstream O-methylation and P450 oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: condenses a fatty acyl-CoA-derived starter to form the alkylresorcinol aromatic core of the sorgoleone pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: coexpression with the other four pathway genes enabled sorgoleone production and necrotic lesions in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Agrobacterium-mediated transient coexpression; partial construct testing (SbARS2+SbOMT3 and SbARS2+SbOMT3+CYP71AM1); RT-qPCR; GC/MS; lesion phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in agroinfiltrated leaves; detectable by RT-qPCR as early as 2 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in leaves; lesion monitored from 3-6 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: constitutive promoters; different promoters/terminators used in multigene construct to avoid interference/silencing
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of agroinfiltrated N. benthamiana leaves, NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA660791
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbARS2 is a central catalytic gene in the assembled sorgoleone pathway. 2. Partial expression with SbOMT3 and CYP71AM1 caused slight lesion-like symptoms, suggesting formation of sorgoleone-like analogs from endogenous substrates. 3. Full coexpression with the desaturases was needed for strong lesion formation and GC/MS-confirmed sorgoleone accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: expression as part of complete pathway caused host leaf necrosis; partial pathway may generate analogs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: important core enzyme for engineering sorgoleone biosynthesis; should be stacked with upstream desaturases and downstream tailoring enzymes under restricted promoters
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion summary mentions SbARS1 in one sentence, but experimental constructs/results throughout the paper use SbARS2

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: phytotoxic allelochemical; inhibits photosystem II/photosynthetic electron flow; contributes to weed suppression
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: O-methylated alkylresorcinol intermediate en route to sorgoleone
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: methylates the aromatic hydroxyl group(s) of the alkylresorcinol intermediate in the sorgoleone pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required within the full pathway for sorgoleone production and severe necrotic lesions; with SbARS2 and CYP71AM1 may contribute to production of lesion-causing analogs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Agrobacterium-mediated transient coexpression; partial construct testing; RT-qPCR; GC/MS; lesion phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in agroinfiltrated leaves; detectable by RT-qPCR as early as 2 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in leaves; lesion monitored from 3-6 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: constitutive promoters; different promoters/terminators used in multigene construct to avoid interference/silencing
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of agroinfiltrated N. benthamiana leaves, NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA660791
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbOMT3 is one of the five functionally assembled sorgoleone biosynthetic enzymes expressed in N. benthamiana. 2. Partial pathway expression including SbOMT3, SbARS2, and CYP71AM1 caused mild lesion-like symptoms, consistent with production of analogs. 3. In the complete pathway, SbOMT3 contributes to authentic sorgoleone accumulation associated with strong phytotoxicity.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: complete pathway expression caused severe necrosis in host leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: required for final pathway reconstruction in crop engineering, ideally under tissue-specific expression to avoid autotoxic effects

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: phytotoxic allelochemical; inhibits photosystem II/photosynthetic electron flow; contributes to weed suppression
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: terminal oxidative conversion to benzoquinone product
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: O-methylated alkylresorcinol precursor in the sorgoleone pathway
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: sorgoleone or immediate oxidized benzoquinone product leading to sorgoleone
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes terminal oxidative tailoring of the aromatic intermediate to generate the benzoquinone structure of sorgoleone
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: part of the full five-gene pathway required for robust sorgoleone accumulation and necrotic lesions in N. benthamiana; expressed alone it did not cause lesions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression of CYP71AM1 alone and in combinations; RT-qPCR; GC/MS; lesion phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transiently expressed in agroinfiltrated leaves; detectable by RT-qPCR as early as 2 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: endoplasmic reticulum (inferred for cytochrome P450; exact localization not directly shown in this paper)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in leaves; lesion monitored from 3-6 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: constitutive promoters; different promoters/terminators used in multigene construct to avoid interference/silencing
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of agroinfiltrated N. benthamiana leaves, NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA660791
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71AM1 was included as the terminal oxidative enzyme in the reconstructed sorgoleone pathway. 2. Expression of CYP71AM1 alone did not cause significant lesions, showing it is insufficient by itself to generate toxic product levels. 3. Coexpression with SbARS2 and SbOMT3 gave slight lesion-like symptoms, while coexpression with the full five-gene set produced GC/MS-detectable sorgoleone and strong necrosis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: complete pathway expression caused severe phytotoxicity/necrotic lesions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: necessary terminal enzyme for engineering de novo sorgoleone biosynthesis; should be combined with tissue-specific deployment to minimize toxicity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors suggest the pathway may operate in the ER because none of the five biosynthetic proteins have chloroplast transit peptides
